title: Massive Slovakian Lemongrass-takrai Bowl
description: A hearty, fermented Slovakian dish that combines the flavors of lemongrass and takrai with roasted vegetables and crunchy fermented elements.
instructions: In a large pot, combine water, flour, and caraway seeds. Whisk until smooth and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
In a separate pan, heat coconut oil over medium heat. Add onion, bell pepper, carrot, celery, and garlic. Cook until tender.
Add cooked vegetables to the pot with the flour mixture. Stir to combine.
Add lemongrass and takrai to the pot. Stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a mason jar or a large ceramic bowl. Cover with a lid or plastic wrap.
Let the mixture ferment at room temperature for 24-48 hours. Check for fermentation by looking for bubbles and a sour smell.
Once fermented, refrigerate the mixture for at least 2 hours to chill.
Serve the massive Slovakian lemongrass-takrai bowl chilled, garnished with additional lemongrass and takrai if desired.
Store any leftover in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.
